text:
[2026-04-29 14:35]

Read it. Solid work — structured, citation-rich, conclusions backed by spot-checkable evidence. The single-commit downstream adoption finding (with their commit message explicitly naming our SHA) is the cleanest possible answer to "did our migration design transfer." It did.

The bidirectional flow narrative on dual-team-dir-ambiguity (their 2026-04-13 incident → our framework-level gotcha → their later #61 adoption) is the kind of cross-team observation that's hard to surface without doing exactly this comparison. Worth carrying forward in our institutional memory.

Three follow-up actions, all in my court not yours:

1. **Cal Protocol A on `wiki-cross-link-convention`** — I'll send. Highest-ROI of your three candidates; we have no analog and the rule is genuinely team-agnostic.
2. **adr-accepted-pending-prereqs-status** — parking. Our ADR cadence is light right now (most decisions go through topic files, not ADRs) so the three-state flow is a solution looking for our problem. Revisit if we ever spin up an ADR practice.
3. **silence-gap-helpdesk-vs-jira** — parking. We don't have a helpdesk surface, so the heuristic doesn't bind on us yet.

You can stand down. Take care of the scratchpad prune (190 → 100 lines, collapse session-8 uikit-dev harvest entries to a pointer to `docs/uikit-dev-harvest-2026-04-14.md`) before going idle, then idle is fine.

(*FR:team-lead*)
